title: KX-02: Chat execution experience — tool activity, approvals, artifacts, retries
description: Upgrade the chat experience with tool-call cards (collapsible per-tool with status, duration, result), approval gates (inline card with approve/decline), interrupt/resume, artifact cards in-stream, and accessible state announcements. Derived from assistant-ui tool cards (MIT), agent-canvas approval UX (MIT), and Pipali retry patterns (Apache-2.0).

packets.title: Tool-call cards: collapsible per-tool with status, duration, result preview
packets.objective: Replace the current inline tool-result text with collapsible tool-call cards. Each tool call gets a card showing: tool name + icon, status (running/done/failed), duration, and expandable result. Streaming tool calls show a spinner and update inline. Harvested pattern from assistant-ui/assistant-ui (MIT). Write ToolCallCard in shared/. Hook into the existing tool result rendering in ChatMessage.tsx.
acceptance_criteria: Tool calls render as collapsible cards, not inline text
Running state shows spinner; done state shows check + duration; failed shows error + retry
Result content is expandable/collapsible
Streaming tool calls update inline without flicker
make ui-test + npm run build pass

packets.title: Approval gates: inline card with approve/decline, pause on pending
packets.objective: When Kitty needs approval, render an inline ApprovalGate card instead of a raw prompt. The card shows: what Kitty wants to do, details, and approve/decline buttons. The conversation pauses until resolved. After approval, the action continues. After decline, conversation resumes with preference recorded. Harvested from agent-canvas (MIT) approval UX.
acceptance_criteria: Approval requests render as inline cards, not raw text
Conversation pauses with a visible 'waiting for approval' indicator
Approve continues the action; Decline records preference and resumes
Keyboard accessible (Enter on Approve, Escape on Decline)
make ui-test + npm run build pass

packets.title: Artifact cards in chat stream: images, documents, code, reports
packets.objective: Render ArtifactCard for generated outputs in-stream. When the backend returns an artifact reference, render the appropriate ArtifactCard type (image=thumbnail, document=excerpt, code=preview, report=summary). Each card shows provenance (which tool/model created it). Actions: view full, download, copy (code).
acceptance_criteria: Artifact references in chat render as ArtifactCards, not bare links
Each type renders with correct visual model per the plan
Provenance is visible (created by tool X at time Y)
View/download/copy actions work
make ui-test + npm run build pass

packets.title: Retry and recovery: per-message retry, interruption resume, error recovery
packets.objective: Replace the current single-message retry with per-tool and per-message retry. Failed tool calls get a retry button that re-runs just that tool. Interrupted messages get retry and continue options. Gateway connectivity errors show a slim persistent banner (not blocking modal) with retry. Every error links to the specific control, never a generic toast. Harvested from Pipali retry patterns (Apache-2.0).
acceptance_criteria: Failed tool call shows retry button that re-runs the specific tool
Interrupted message shows retry and continue options
Gateway offline shows slim banner, not modal
Every error message names what failed and how to recover
make ui-test + npm run build pass

packets.title: Accessible live status announcements for streaming and state changes
packets.objective: Add aria-live regions to announce streaming content updates and state changes to screen readers. The main content area gets aria-live='polite' for normal streaming. aria-live='assertive' for errors, approvals needed, and gateway-down states. StatusBadge gets role='status' for automatic announcement.
acceptance_criteria: Streaming content is announced to screen readers via polite region
Errors and approval requests are announced via assertive region
StatusBadge announces state changes automatically
make ui-test + npm run build pass
